channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Dell Overhauls Computer Naming Schema: A New Era of Simplicity
item.description: Dell announces a significant change in its computer naming schema, replacing distinctive brands with more intuitive options. The move aims to simplify the shopping experience for customers and leverage the company's existing brand equity.
item.dc:creator: Katy Steinmetz, Fast Company

item.title: Sony PlayStation is adding smell—yes, you read that right—to its games
item.description: Sony has unveiled a new gaming system that could allow PlayStation players to sniff their way through games like The Last of Us. Unveiled at CES 2025, the Future Immersive Entertainment Concept (FIEC) features a huge, room-size setup designed to push the boundaries of immersive gaming.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: What to know as TikTok argues in front of Supreme Court
item.description: TikTok is hoping to invalidate a law that would force it to divest from its Chinese parent company or face a ban.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Musk-Trump relationship not a 'concern': Hyundai CEO
item.description: Hyundai CEO José Muñoz said he’s not concerned about the relationship between President-elect Trump and Tesla CEO Elon Musk, whose company is one of Hyundai’s biggest competitors. Instead, Munoz said in an interview with Bloomberg that he thinks their close relationship could benefit the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Electric Vehicle Demand Rises, But Policy Uncertainty Clouds Forecast
item.description: Global sales of battery electric vehicles expected to reach 15.1 million by 2025, but uncertainty over policy changes and tariffs poses a threat to the industry.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Assassin's Creed Shadows gets delayed again, as Ubisoft pushes the game back by nearly five weeks
item.description: Assassin's Creed Shadows has been delayed again with the next entry in the behemoth series pushed back to March 20, 2025.
item.dc:creator: rob.dwiar@futurenet.com (Rob Dwiar), TechRadar

item.title: You can now get a Raspberry Pi 5 with 16GB RAM
item.description: 16GB RAM Raspberry Pi 5 model joins its budget 2GB counterpart to use-cases such as AI workloads and smooth desktop use.
item.dc:creator: luke.hughes@futurenet.com (Luke Hughes), TechRadar

item.title: MSI could be cooking up a Ryzen Z2-powered Claw to take on gaming handhelds
item.description: It seems like MSI isn't holding back, if this rumor regarding a potential AMD Ryzen Z2 Claw handheld gaming PC is true.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: Companies across the globe, at least 4 in 10, are likely to cut jobs as artificial intelligence (AI) continues to advance, new research shows.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Digital Transformation: From Optimization to Experience Innovation
item.description: Digital transformation has been the mantra for businesses everywhere, yet many organizations have reached a plateau. Discover how experience innovation can help companies move beyond optimization and into the realm of value creation.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Los CDO pasan a ser el centro de atención de las estrategias empresariales
item.description: Dado que los datos son fundamentales para todos los aspectos de la empresa, el director de Datos se ha convertido en un ejecutivo altamente estratégico. El CDO de hoy se centra en ayudar a la organización a aprovechar los datos como un activo empresarial para impulsar los resultados.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Lawmakers to seek GAO review of TSA’s biometrics, facial recognition use
item.description: TSA has expanded its use of facial recognition technology to more than 80 airports across the country in recent years. The post Lawmakers to seek GAO review of TSA’s biometrics, facial recognition use first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Los CIO están replanteándose cómo utilizan los servicios de nube pública
item.description: En los últimos años, las empresas se han esforzado por trasladar la mayor cantidad posible de recursos lo más rápido posible a la nube pública para minimizar los gastos de capital y ahorrar dinero. Sin embargo, cada vez más, los CIO están revisando y racionalizando dichas inversiones.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to use the new Lock object in C# 13
item.description: The C# programming language has provided support for thread synchronization using the lock keyword since its earliest versions. By using a lock statement, you ensure that only one thread can execute the body of the statement at a time. Any other thread is blocked until the lock is released.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The ‘Worst in Show’ CES products put your data at risk and cause waste, privacy advocates say
item.description: So much of the technology showcased at CES includes gadgets made to improve consumers’ lives — whether by leveraging AI to make devices that help people become more efficient, by creating companions to cure loneliness or by providing tools that help people with mental and physical health.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 9 buoni propositi IT per il 2025
item.description: I CIO sono molto ambiziosi. Per l’anno appena iniziato, non sembrano volersi accontentare dello status quo e, al contrario, fissano grandi obiettivi, secondo innumerevoli sondaggi realizzati di recente sui dirigenti IT.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta tests eBay listings in Facebook Marketplace to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Why I’m making 2025 my year of dead-tree books
item.description: Over the past 17 years or so, I have happily paid for hundreds of e-books—everything from reference works to history to novels to comics. Having immediate access to all of them on a device that fits in my pocket ranks among my favorite miracles of the digital age.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: GlassFish update fixes restart hangs, random 403 responses
item.description: The Eclipse Foundation has released GlassFish 7.0.21, an update of the enterprise Java application server that focuses on fixing a few tenacious bugs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn’t just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal cyber leaders on building national resilience
item.description: How are you building resilience into your agency’s cybersecurity? The post Federal cyber leaders on building national resilience first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Some Facebook users now allowed to see eBay listings in Marketplace
item.description: Meta on Wednesday announced some Facebook users will be able to view eBay listings on its Marketplace features just months after the European Commission slammed the social media company with a hefty anti-competition fine.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Silicon Valley homeless nonprofit sues Microsoft over disrupted email access
item.description: The nonprofit LifeMoves claims the disruption has made it difficult to connect homeless people with services.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Varian, The Mercury News

item.title: Meta's fact-checking changes put company under political microscope
item.description: Meta’s decision to eliminate its fact-checking program marks a sharp reversal for the major social media company, quickly prompting questions over the firm’s direction as President-elect Trump heads back to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Federal IT skills shortage: How AI can help relieve the gap and improve security
item.description: The global IT skills shortage is predicted to impact 90% of organizations by 2026, according to IDC. The post Federal IT skills shortage: How AI can help relieve the gap and improve security first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities
item.description: The White House is prepping a new cybersecurity executive order that would address cloud security, give CISA new authorities to do threat hunting and more. The post White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta's new board includes UFC boss Dana White, a familiar figure in Trump's orbit
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Appeals court blocks return of US net neutrality rules for ISPs
item.description: The reintroduction of neutrality rules for network providers is looking increasingly unlikely, at least for the next five years, after a US appeals court blocked efforts by the US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to reinstate them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Write Python like it’s 2025
item.description: It’s a new year, and we feel fine! 2024 was a breakout year for our favorite programming language, and we have a feeling 2025 will be even bigger.
item.dc:creator: Info World
